# US Stock Watcher Next-Session Forecast — Thursday 7/23

**[Thursday 7/23 session outlook] Bullish lean — Moderate / selective risk-on · composite +26 · confidence 43%**

**Next-session forecast:** The next U.S. regular session is Thursday, July 23, opening at 9:30 AM ET (22:30 KST on 7/23). Everything that follows is a forward-looking outlook for that upcoming session, prepared while the U.S. market is closed.

*Data as of: 2026-07-22T22:17:06 · Data quality: fresh*

## Executive Summary

Heading into Thursday's open, the composite market-state score stands at +26 out of 100, placing the setup in "Moderate / selective risk-on" territory with a confidence reading of 43% across four measured components. Several inputs remain unmeasured for this cycle — liquidity, credit, earnings, macro surprises, valuation, and positioning were reweighted out of the current read — so the signal should be treated as directionally useful but not exhaustive. On balance, the tilt into Thursday's session favors a constructive, selectively risk-on posture.

## Market Diagnosis

**Indices.** Going into the next session, the major benchmarks closed as follows: the S&P 500 at 7,498.96 (-0.14% on the day), the Nasdaq-100 at 28,998.10 (-0.54%), the Dow at 52,218.58 (-0.01%), and the Russell 2000 at 2,959.94 (-0.92%). The broader tape's modest softness, led by small-caps, sets the technical backdrop for Thursday's open.

**Market drivers.** Of the four factors feeding the composite score, three are supportive heading into Thursday — volatility, index trend, and the yield curve — while the US dollar stands out as the lone headwind.

**Breadth & concentration.** The overall market-state score of +26/100 corresponds to the "Moderate / selective risk-on" classification, carried with 43% confidence on four measured components. As noted above, liquidity, credit, earnings, macro surprises, valuation, and positioning were not part of this measurement cycle.

**Sector & rotation.** Leadership into Thursday is concentrated in Energy, Health Care, and Financials, while Materials, Industrials, and Technology are lagging — a rotation profile worth watching for continuation or reversal at the next open.

**Rates, dollar & commodities.** The 10-year Treasury yield sits at 4.63%. The dollar index (DXY) is at 101.14, down 0.04% on the day. In commodities, WTI crude has jumped 3.46% to 87.85, and gold has advanced 1.35% to 4,126.00 — both notable moves to carry into Thursday's cross-asset backdrop.

## Outlook

**Short term.** Framed specifically for Thursday's 9:30 AM ET open, the near-term bias is constructive, consistent with the "Moderate / selective risk-on" market state at 43% confidence. This view would flip toward a defensive lean should the overall composite score cross back below zero.

**Medium term.** The medium-term stance carries the same constructive bias, anchored to the same "Moderate / selective risk-on" reading and 43% confidence level, with the identical trigger for reassessment: a cross below zero on the overall score.

**Medium-to-long term.** Over the medium-to-long horizon, the read remains constructive as well, again tied to the "Moderate / selective risk-on" state and 43% confidence, subject to the same zero-line flip condition on the composite score.

## Top News

Ahead of Thursday's session, the news flow investors will be digesting includes: Nvidia's rebound offering relief even as tougher questions swirl around an AI bubble ("NVDA Stock Outlook: Nvidia Rebound Offers Relief but AI Bubble Faces Tougher Questions," FXLeaders); a bearish call on Apple ahead of its Q3 print ("Apple: Sell Before Q3 (NASDAQ:AAPL)," Seeking Alpha); ServiceNow's (NOW) strong Q2 earnings and raised revenue outlook (GuruFocus); reports that Apple (AAPL) is planning a major Mac line upgrade amid surging AI demand (GuruFocus); Foxconn rehiring workers for iPhone 18 production as Apple raises its AI stakes (Yellow.com); and Alphabet's CFO signaling that demand still outpaces capacity, lifting AI infrastructure stocks (Benzinga).
